name: Markdown.Elm

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Parse the input and render it to Html, e.g.,

toHtml ExtendedMath "Pythagoras said: $a^2 + b^2 c^2$."

values.name: toHtmlWithExternaTOC
values.comment:
Like `toHtmlWithTOC`, but constructs returns a record,
one field of which is the rendered document,
anther of which is the rendered table of contents.
values.type: Markdown.Option.Option -> String.String -> { title : Html.Html msg, toc : Html.Html msg, document : Html.Html msg }

values.name: toHtmlWithTOC
values.comment: Like `toHtml`, but constructs a table of contents.

name: Markdown.ElmWithId
comment:
Use this module if you need to edit math + markdown _and_
require optimizations for speed and a smooth editing experience.

The
function `parse` yields a syntax tree (AST: abstract syntax tree). The functions `renderHtml`,
`renderHtmlWithTOC`, and `renderHtmlWithExternaTOC` render the
AST in various forms, as described below.


## Optimizations

The idea of the optimizations used in `app-demo-optimized`
(see the repo)
is to parse the document text when the
document is first opened. The resulting parse
tree is stored as
`model.lastAst`. Each block in the AST carries
a label `(version, id): (Int, Int)`, where
the `id` is unique to each block.
Each time the text changes, a new AST is computed
with an incremented version number. The
function `Diff.mergeWith equals` is applied to
the old and new ASTs
to compute an updated AST. The updated AST
is identical to the new AST except for the id's.
The id of a node in the updated AST is
the same as in the old AST if and only if the type, level,
and content of the node has not changed.
This information is used to signal MathJax not
to re-render mathematical text that is unchanged.

To see where these optimizations are applied,
look for the places in `app-demo-optimized/Main.elm`
where functions in the modules
`ParseWithId` and `Markdown.ElmWithId` are called.


## Rendering

@docs renderHtml, toHtml, renderHtmlWithTOC, renderHtmlWithExternaTOC


## Utility

@docs numberOfMathElements

values.name: numberOfMathElements
values.comment: Count the number of display math element blocks in the parse tree

values.name: renderHtml
values.comment: Render a parse tree to Html.

values.name: renderHtmlWithExternaTOC
values.comment:
Like `renderHtmlWithTOC`, but transforms a parser tree into a record,
with fields for the document title, the table of contents, and the body
of the document.
values.type: Markdown.Parse.Id -> String.String -> Tree.Tree Markdown.Parse.MDBlockWithId -> { title : Html.Html msg, toc : Html.Html msg, document : Html.Html msg }

values.name: renderHtmlWithTOC
values.comment: Like `renderHtml`, but constructs a table of contents.
values.type: Markdown.Parse.Id -> String.String -> Tree.Tree Markdown.Parse.MDBlockWithId -> Html.Html msg

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Parse the input and render it to Html, e.g.,

toHtml ExtendedMath "Pythagoras said: $a^2 + b^2 c^2$."
values.type: Markdown.Parse.Id -> Basics.Int -> Markdown.Option.Option -> String.String -> Html.Html msg

name: Markdown.Option

unions.name: Option
unions.comment:
**Description:**

  - Standard: vanilla Markdown
  - Extended: strike-through text, poetry and verbatim blocks
  - ExtendMath: like Extended, but TeX formulas are rendered

name: Markdown.Parse
comment:
The purpose of this module is to parse a Document,
that is, a string, into an abstract syntax tree (AST)
which can then be further transformed or passed on
to a rendering function. The AST is a rose tree
of `MDBlockWithId` — short for "Markdown Blocks."

See the documentation at the head of module `Markdown.ElmWithId` for
the rationale for this module.


## Create or use AST

@docs toMDBlockTree, searchAST, sourceMap, getLeadingTextFromAST


## Types

@docs MDBlock, MDBlockWithId, BlockContent, Id


## Ids

@docs getId, idFromString, stringFromId, idOfBlock


## Comparison

@docs equalContent, equalIds


## Tools

@docs project, projectedStringOfBlockContent, stringOfMDBlockTree

unions.name: BlockContent
unions.comment: The type of a parsed Block

unions.name: MDBlock
unions.comment:
An MDBlock (Markdown block) carries

    - the type of the block
    - its level
    - its unparsed content

unions.name: MDBlockWithId
unions.comment:
An MBlockWithId is like na MDBlock,
except that it has an

    Id : (Int, Int)

which should be thought of as

    ( version, id )

where the id is unique to each block.

aliases.name: Id
aliases.comment:
Used to generate Ids of Html elements and to
implement differential rendering. The first
Int is a version number, incremented after each
edit. The second is a an integer representing
position in a traversal of the tree of blocks
obtained by parsing the text.

values.name: equalContent
values.comment:
Check for equality of

    - blockType
    - level
    - content

ignoring the id.

values.name: equalIds
values.comment: Return true of the blocks have the same id

values.name: getId
values.comment:
Given a string s, return (ss, id), where ss is a string containing s
and id a Maybe value representing the id of the corresponding element
in the rendered text.
values.type: String.String -> Dict.Dict String.String String.String -> ( String.String, Maybe.Maybe String.String )

values.name: getLeadingTextFromAST
values.comment: Get lead ing text element from AST

values.name: idFromString
values.comment:
Compute id from a string representation of that id, e.g..

    idFromString "i5v3"
    --> (5,3)

values.name: idOfBlock
values.comment: Return the id of a block

values.name: project
values.comment:
Project an MDBlockWithId to an MDBlock
by omitting its id

values.name: projectedStringOfBlockContent
values.comment:
Return a string representing the content of a block
if it is of type `T`. Otherwise, return the empty string.

values.name: searchAST
values.comment:
Search the AST for nodes whose label contains the
given string, returning the Id of the first node found,
if any.

values.name: sourceMap
values.comment:
Create a sourceMap from the AST: a dictionary whose keys
are text strings and whose values
ids of the corresponding elements in the DOM

values.name: stringFromId
values.comment:
Compute a string representation of an id. Thus

    stringFromId (5,3)
    -> "i5v3"

values.name: stringOfMDBlockTree
values.comment:
A string representation of an MDBlockTree. Useful
for verifying the validity of the AST.

values.name: toMDBlockTree
values.comment:
Parse a string using a Markdown flavor option, returning the AST.
Example:

    Parse.toMDBlockTree 1 Extended "This **is** a test."
    --> Tree (MDBlockWithId (0,1)
    -->    (MarkdownBlock Root) 0 (M (Paragraph [
    -->       Line [OrdinaryText "DOCUMENT"]]))) [
    -->          Tree (MDBlockWithId (1,1)
    -->            (MarkdownBlock Plain) 1 (M (Paragraph [
    -->                Line [  OrdinaryText ("This ")
    -->              , BoldText ("is ")
    -->              , OrdinaryText ("a test.")]
    -->              , Line []
    -->            ])))
    -->      []]
    --> : Tree.Tree Parse.MDBlockWithId
